about.tagline: A desktop practice room — built for anyone who wants to sing, truly hear their own voice, and make every rehearsal audibly better
about.disclosure.title: Disclosure
about.disclosure.body: The author has no prior programming background. This project was built through AI collaboration (Claude / Codex) — architecture, code, and UI were all AI-generated. All features have been tested and cross-model reviewed (Claude for implementation, Codex for independent audit). Please evaluate the risk against your own use case before adopting.
about.notes.title: Getting Started
about.workflow.title: Recommended workflow
about.workflow.1: Import a backing track on the Setup tab, or download one from YouTube.
about.workflow.2: If you have the original song, separate vocals.wav first with UVR5 / Moises.
about.workflow.3: Import vocals.wav in Pitch Curve Source; the app creates the pitch curve and enables guide vocal monitoring.
about.workflow.4: Choose your microphone and headphones, then run latency calibration on first use.
about.workflow.5: On the Recording tab, balance backing, microphone, and guide vocal volume before recording.
about.workflow.6: Play back the take, then export a vocal or mix WAV.
about.notes.1.title: Prepare a backing track
about.notes.1.body: Load an off-vocal / instrumental file (MP3, WAV, M4A, etc.) or download it directly from YouTube. Avoid versions with the original vocal, which interferes with recording.
about.notes.2.title: Pitch curve source (optional)
about.notes.2.body: If you want a reference line on the pitch curve, you can provide one via:
about.notes.2.li1.after: to separate a clean vocals track from the original, then import it
about.notes.2.li2: Load a MIDI file as the melody reference
about.notes.3.title: Guide vocal monitoring
about.notes.3.body: After importing a clean vocals track, the Recording tab shows a guide vocal volume row. The guide is headphone monitoring only, helping you follow the melody while recording, and is not mixed into exported vocal / mix files.
about.notes.4.title: Latency calibration
about.notes.4.body: First-time users should run the latency calibration (bottom of the Setup page) so the recording timeline aligns precisely with the backing track. The value is saved automatically.
about.notes.5.title: Lyrics sync
about.notes.5.body: Supports LRC / SRT / VTT. After downloading YouTube subtitles you can load them directly as lyrics, or manually mark timestamps in the sync editor.
about.shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
about.license.title: License
about.license.main.body1: VocalSync Studio is open-sourced under the
about.license.main.body2: . You are free to use, modify, and distribute the software without warranty.
about.license.main.note: The CREPE pitch-detection model used by this software is developed by NYU MARL and also MIT-licensed. Major frameworks and libraries such as Tauri, Svelte, Symphonia, cpal, and rustfft are MIT / Apache-2.0.
about.license.ytdlp.heading: yt-dlp (Unlicense)
about.license.ytdlp.body.after: (effectively public domain). This program calls an unmodified yt-dlp.exe as a CLI subprocess, without static linking. Whether content downloaded via yt-dlp complies with local copyright law and YouTube's terms of service is the user's responsibility.
about.license.ffmpeg.heading: FFmpeg (LGPL 2.1+ / possibly GPL)
about.license.ffmpeg.body.after: licensing; enabling specific encoders such as libx264 / libx265 switches it to GPL-2.0-or-later. This program invokes ffmpeg / ffprobe as a CLI subprocess without static linking against libav* libraries; no FFmpeg binaries are bundled in this repository. Please check the specific license version of your build (essentials builds are typically LGPL; full builds include GPL components).
about.license.disclaimer: Disclaimer: This tool is intended solely as a local singing-practice companion. Copyright responsibility and lawful use of any audio content processed or downloaded with this tool rests entirely with the user. The developer assumes no responsibility for misuse.
about.creator.title: Creator
about.creator.desc: If this tool helps your practice, you are warmly welcome to buy me a coffee so development can continue.
about.feedback.title: Feedback
about.feedback.desc: Bug reports, feature suggestions, and general questions are all welcome. You can reach me in two ways:
about.links.title: Links & Updates
